title: Add Trust Layer schema foundation and validators
outOfScope: Human decision runtime protocol and Preflight integration
Cockpit Status presentation changes beyond generated active status

sources.reason: Task 1 defines the five Trust Layer data contracts, validators, and acceptance boundaries.

sources.path: user-provided/AI Cockpit Trust Layer Upgrade specification
sources.reason: Defines the required fields and protocol semantics for Capability, Success Criteria, Human Decision, and Baseline evidence.
Defines the Contract, Summary, verification, and lifecycle requirements for this Work Item.
Defines Evidence Governance and the boundary between native and delegated evidence.

problemStatement: The Trust Layer needs machine-validated, versioned data contracts before guards and runtime decisions can safely consume capability, success, human-decision, and baseline evidence.

riskAssessment.reason: Schemas become a shared contract for later guards and evidence producers; invalid or ambiguous shapes must fail deterministically without implementing later Trust Layer behavior.

executionDecision.reason: The schema fields and validation boundary are explicit and limited to Task 1.

preReviewWarnings: Review schema strictness, required fields, versioning, and deterministic rejection of malformed evidence before approving the next guard task.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: Five versioned JSON Schemas exist for repository capabilities, success criteria, Human Decision Request, Human Decision Evidence, and baseline evidence.
A standard-library validator validates every schema example and rejects missing required fields, wrong types, unknown top-level fields, and invalid enum values deterministically.

Human Decision Request validation requires decision identity, state, risk category, what happened, why it matters, evidence, options with consequences, recommendation, recommendation reason, question, and resume condition.
Human Decision Evidence validation requires decision identity, selected option, decision text, actor, timestamp, rationale, source, and supersession semantics.
Automated tests cover positive examples, malformed payloads, schema version mismatches, and the minimum required evidence fields; `make check-trust-schemas` is available and included in quality.
guidelines: Keep schemas language-neutral and use explicit machine-readable field names.
Reject malformed or unknown top-level fields rather than silently interpreting them.
Do not implement guard decisions or runtime integration in this schema foundation task.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: All five valid Trust Layer schema examples pass validation
scenarioCoverage.reason: This is a post-implementation test scenario, not an implementation-readiness decision gate; make check-trust-schemas and pytest provide the evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Missing required Human Decision Request fields fail validation without inference
scenarioCoverage.reason: This is a post-implementation test scenario, not an implementation-readiness decision gate; malformed-payload tests provide the evidence.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: Schema version and unknown top-level field mismatches fail deterministically
scenarioCoverage.reason: This is a post-implementation test scenario, not an implementation-readiness decision gate; validator tests provide the evidence.

intent.problem: Later Trust Layer guards need stable, machine-validated data contracts for repository capability, success criteria, human decisions, and baseline evidence; the repository currently has no shared schema foundation for those records.
constraints: Keep the contracts language-neutral and deterministic.
Use standard-library validation only; do not add a runtime dependency.
Reject malformed or unknown top-level data instead of inferring intent.
Do not implement guard decisions or Preflight integration in Task 1.
intent.rationale: A small, strict schema layer gives later guard and evidence Work Items a common contract while keeping policy and runtime behavior out of the foundation task.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: The user explicitly authorized execution of the complete Trust Layer plan in the current conversation; scope remains limited to the declared schema foundation files.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
